sources.title: The safety of green tea and green tea extract consumption in adults - Results of a systematic review
Efficacy and Safety of Curcumin and Curcuma longa Extract in the Treatment of Arthritis: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trial

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: limited human safety evidence and rare case reports. Concentrated, catechin-rich green tea extracts can cause liver injury, with risk influenced by dose and fasting exposure; this does not apply automatically to brewed tea or food use.
changes.rationale: Adds an evidence-labelled, extract-specific runtime summary while preserving the workbook's existing liver-risk warning and formulation limits.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: short-term human trials provide limited, preparation-specific safety data. Mild gastrointestinal effects can occur; the existing workbook cautions for bleeding-risk therapy, surgery, biliary disease, pregnancy, and breastfeeding remain precautionary and require individualized review.
changes.rationale: Adds an evidence-labelled runtime summary without upgrading precautionary workbook cautions into established interaction claims.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: limited human safety evidence and rare case reports. Isolated or concentrated EGCG can cause liver injury, particularly with high-dose or fasting exposure; evidence does not establish a universally safe supplemental dose.
changes.rationale: Preserves the isolated-extract distinction and explicitly labels the evidence and dosing uncertainty.

changes.new_value: Avoid high-dose or fasting use and seek clinician review with pre-existing liver disease or unexplained liver-test abnormalities; concentrated EGCG safety is preparation- and dose-specific.
changes.rationale: Replaces a token with readable, formulation-specific liver-risk guidance from the systematic safety review.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: limited human safety evidence and rare case reports. Concentrated green tea or EGCG extracts can cause liver injury, particularly with high-dose or fasting exposure; caffeine content and risk vary by preparation.
changes.rationale: Adds an evidence-labelled summary that preserves dose, fasting, and preparation uncertainty for this mixed extract profile.

changes.new_value: Avoid high-dose or fasting use and seek clinician review with pre-existing liver disease or unexplained liver-test abnormalities; verify caffeine content if stimulant-sensitive.
changes.rationale: Replaces an unlabeled severity token with the profile's existing liver, fasting, and stimulant-sensitivity distinctions.

changes.new_value: Use caution with anticoagulant or antiplatelet therapy, before surgery, and with gallbladder or bile-duct disease; avoid unsupervised high supplemental doses during pregnancy or breastfeeding because safety evidence is limited.
changes.rationale: Converts tokens into readable precautionary guidance while preserving uncertainty and avoiding a medication-interaction schema claim.
